(lx:(list 'a & 'a)) -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (length (snoc lx) = length (fst lx) + 1)) (** Reverse induction principle **)
p:(list 'a -> Tot bool) -> l:list 'a -> Lemma (requires ((p []) /\ (forall hd tl. p hd ==> p (hd@[tl])))) (ensures (p l)) (** Properties about iterators **)
f:('a -> Tot 'b) -> l:(list 'a) -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (length (map f l)) = length l) [SMTPat (map f l)] (** Properties about unsnoc *) (** [unsnoc] is the inverse of [snoc] *)
#a:Type -> l:list a{length l > 0} -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (snoc (unsnoc l) == l)) [SMTPat (snoc (unsnoc l))] (** [snoc] is the inverse of [unsnoc] *)
#a:Type -> lx:(list a & a) -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (unsnoc (snoc lx) == lx)) [SMTPat (unsnoc (snoc lx))] (** Doing an [unsnoc] gives us a list that is shorter in length by 1 *)
#a:Type -> l:list a{length l > 0} -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (length (fst (unsnoc l)) == length l - 1)) (** [unsnoc] followed by [append] can be connected to the same vice-versa. *)

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> Tot bool) -> l:list a -> x:a -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (let l1, l2 = partition f l in mem x l = (mem x l1 || mem x l2))) (** Same as [partition_mem], but using [forall] *)

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> Tot bool) -> l:list a -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (let l1, l2 = partition f l in (forall x. mem x l = (mem x l1 || mem x l2)))) (** If [partition f l = (l1, l2)], then for any [x], if [x] is in [l1] (resp. [l2]), then [f x] holds (resp. does not hold) *)

#a:eqtype -> p:(a -> Tot bool) -> l:list a -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (let l1, l2 = partition p l in (forall x. mem x l1 ==> p x) /\ (forall x. mem x l2 ==> not (p x)))) (** If [partition f l = (l1, l2)], then the number of occurrences of any [x] in [l] is the same as the sum of the number of occurrences in [l1] and [l2]. *)

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> Tot bool) -> l:list a -> x:a -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (count x l = (count x (fst (partition f l)) + count x (snd (partition f l))))) (** Same as [partition_count], but using [forall] *)

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> Tot bool) -> l:list a -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (forall x. count x l = (count x (fst (partition f l)) + count x (snd (partition f l))))) (* [SMTPat (partitionT f l)] *) (** Properties about subset **)

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> a -> Tot int) -> l:list a -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (forall x. count x l = count x (sortWith f l))) (** [sorted f l] holds if, and only if, any two consecutive elements [x], [y] of [l] are such that [f x y] holds *)

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> a -> Tot bool) -> l1:list a{sorted f l1} -> l2:list a{sorted f l2} -> pivot:a -> Lemma (requires (total_order #a f /\ (forall y. mem y l1 ==> not(f pivot y)) /\ (forall y. mem y l2 ==> f pivot y))) (ensures (sorted f (l1@(pivot::l2)))) [SMTPat (sorted f (l1@(pivot::l2)))] (** Correctness of [sortWith], part 2/2: the elements of [sortWith f l] are sorted according to comparison

#a:eqtype -> f:(a -> a -> Tot int) -> l:list a -> Lemma (requires (total_order #a (bool_of_compare f))) (ensures ((sorted (bool_of_compare f) (sortWith f l)) /\ (forall x. mem x l = mem x (sortWith f l)))) (** Properties of [noRepeats] *)

#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> m:t key value -> k1:key -> k2:key -> v:value -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (k2=!=k1 ==> sel (upd m k2 v) k1 == sel m k1)) [SMTPat (sel (upd m k2 v) k1)]
#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> v:value -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (sel (const v) k == v)) [SMTPat (sel (const v) k)]

#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> m1:t key value -> m2:t key value -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (contains m2 k ==> sel (concat m1 m2) k==sel m2 k)) [SMTPat (sel (concat m1 m2) k)]
#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> m1:t key value -> m2:t key value -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (not(contains m2 k) ==> sel (concat m1 m2) k==sel m1 k)) [SMTPat (sel (concat m1 m2) k)]
#val1:Type -> #val2:Type -> f:(val1 -> val2) -> #key:eqtype -> m:t key val1 -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (sel (map_val f m) k == f (sel m k))) [SMTPat (sel (map_val f m) k)]

#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> m:t key value -> k1:key -> k2:key -> v:value -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (k2=!=k1 ==> contains (upd m k2 v) k1 == contains m k1)) [SMTPat (contains (upd m k2 v) k1)]
#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> v:value -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (contains (const v) k)) [SMTPat (contains (const v) k)]

#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> m1:t key value -> m2:t key value -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (contains (concat m1 m2) k==(contains m1 k || contains m2 k))) [SMTPat (contains (concat m1 m2) k)]
#val1:Type -> #val2:Type -> f:(val1 -> val2) -> #key:eqtype -> m:t key val1 -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (contains (map_val f m) k == contains m k)) [SMTPat (contains (map_val f m) k)]

#key:eqtype -> #value:Type -> m:t key value -> ks:S.set key -> k:key -> Lemma (requires True) (ensures (contains (restrict ks m) k == (S.mem k ks && contains m k))) [SMTPat (contains (restrict ks m) k)]

a:int -> b:pos -> Lemma (a - b * (a / b) >= 0 /\ a - b * (a / b) < b)
a:int -> b:int -> q:int -> Lemma (requires (a < q)) (ensures (a + q * b < q * (b+1)))
a:nat -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (requires (b <= c)) (ensures (a * b <= a * c))
a:nat -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (requires (b <= c)) (ensures (b * a <= c * a))

a:int -> b:int -> Lemma (a * b = b * a)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma ((a + b) * c = a * c + b * c) (* Lemma: multiplication is left distributive over addition *)

a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a * (b + c) = a * b + a * c) (* Lemma: multiplication is associative, hence parenthesizing is meaningless *) (* GM: This is really just an identity since the LHS is associated to the left *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a * b * c = (a * b) * c) (* Lemma: multiplication is associative, hence parenthesizing is meaningless *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a * b * c = a * (b * c)) (* Lemma: addition is associative, hence parenthesizing is meaningless *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a + b + c = (a + b) + c) (* Lemma: addition is associative, hence parenthesizing is meaningless *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a + b + c = a + (b + c))
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a + b + c = (a + b) + c /\ a + b + c = a + (b + c))
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a - b + c = (a - b) + c /\ a - b - c = a - (b + c) /\ a - b - c = (a - b) - c /\ a + (-b - c) = a - b - c /\ a - (b - c) = a - b + c)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a + b - c = (a - c) + b) (* Lemma: minus applies to the whole term *)
a:int -> b:int -> Lemma (-(a * b) = (-a) * b) (* Lemma: minus applies to the whole term *)
a:int -> b:int -> Lemma (-(a * b) = a * (-b))
a:int -> b:int -> Lemma ((-a) * b = a * (-b)) (* Lemma: multiplication is left distributive over subtraction *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma ((a - b) * c = a * c - b * c) (* Lemma: multiplication is right distributive over subtraction *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma ((a * (b - c) = a * b - a * c)) (* Lemma: multiplication precedence on addition *)
a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a + (b * c) = a + b * c)
a:int -> b:int -> Lemma (abs a * abs b = abs (a * b))

a:int -> b:nat -> c:int -> d:nat -> Lemma (requires (-b < a /\ a < b /\ -d < c /\ c < d)) (ensures (-(b * d) < a * c /\ a * c < b * d)) (* Zero is neutral for addition *)

document (** [doc_of_char c] is a document that consists of the single character [c]. This character must not be a newline. *)
char -> document (** [doc_of_string s] is a document that consists of the string [s]. This string must not contain a newline. *)
string -> document (** [doc_of_bool b] is a document that consists of the boolean [b]. *)
bool -> document (** [substring s ofs len] is a document that consists of the portion of the string [s] delimited by the offset [ofs] and the length [len]. This portion must contain a newline. *)

string -> int -> int -> document (** [fancystring s apparent_length] is a document that consists of the string [s]. This string must not contain a newline. The string may contain fancy characters: color escape characters, UTF-8 or multi-byte characters, etc. Thus, its apparent length (which measures how many columns the text will take up on screen) differs from its length in bytes. *)

string -> int -> document (** [fancysubstring s ofs len apparent_length] is a document that consists of the portion of the string [s] delimited by the offset [ofs] and the length [len]. This portion must not contain a newline. The string may contain fancy characters. *)
string -> int -> int -> int -> document (** [utf8string s] is a document that consists of the UTF-8-encoded string [s]. This string must not contain a newline. *)
string -> document (** [hardline] is a forced newline document. This document forces all enclosing groups to be printed in non-flattening mode. In other words, any enclosing groups are dissolved. *)
document (** [blank n] is a document that consists of [n] blank characters. *)

int -> document (** [break_ n] is a document which consists of either [n] blank characters, when forced to display on a single line, or a single newline character, otherwise. Note that there is no choice at this point: choices are encoded by the [group] combinator. *)
int -> document (** [doc1 ^^ doc2] is the concatenation of the documents [doc1] and [doc2]. *)

int -> document -> document (** [group doc] encodes a choice. If possible, then the entire document [group doc] is rendered on a single line. Otherwise, the group is dissolved, and [doc] is rendered. There might be further groups within [doc], whose presence will lead to further choices being explored. *)

This code is not UTF-8 aware. *)

See tests/bug-reports/Bug2635.fst for some examples Update 11/14/2022: with the introduction of the core typechecker, this primitive should no longer be necessary. Try using the co

unit -> Tac unit (** [ctrl_rewrite] will traverse the current goal, and call [ctrl] * repeatedly on subterms. When [ctrl t] returns [(true, _)], the * tactic will call [rw] with a goal of type [t = ?u], which once * solved will rewrite [t] to the solution of [?u]. No goal is * made if [ctrl t] returns [(false, _)]. * * The second component of the return value of [ctrl] specifies * whether for cont

direction -> (ctrl : term -> Tac (bool & ctrl_flag)) -> (rw:unit -> Tac unit) -> Tac unit (** Given the current goal [Gamma |- w : t], [dup] will turn this goal into [Gamma |- ?u : t] and [Gamma |= ?u == w]. It can thus be used to change a goal's witness in any way needed, by choosing some [?u] (possibly with exact) and then solving the other goal. *)

unit -> Tac unit // Proof namespace management (** [prune "A.B.C"] will mark all top-level definitions in module [A.B.C] (and submodules of it) to not be encoded to the SMT, for the current goal. The string is a namespace prefix. [prune ""] will prune everything, but note that [prune "FStar.S"] will not prune ["FStar.Set"]. *)
string -> Tac unit (** The opposite operation of [prune]. The latest one takes precedence. *)

string -> Tac unit (** Destruct a value of an inductive type by matching on it. The generated match has one branch for each constructor and is therefore trivially exhaustive, no VC is generated for that purpose. It returns a list with the fvars of each constructor and their arities, in the order they appear as goals. *)
term -> Tac (list (fv & nat)) (** Set command line options for the current goal. Mostly useful to change SMT encoding options such as [set_options "--z3rlimit 20"]. *)

string -> Tac unit (** Creates a new, unconstrained unification variable in environment [env]. The type of the uvar can optionally be provided in [o]. If not provided, a second uvar is created for the type. *)

env -> option typ -> Tac term (** Creates a new, unconstrained unification variable in environment [env]. The type of the uvar must be provided and the uvar can be solved to a Ghost term of that type *)
env -> typ -> Tac term (** Creates a new, unconstrained universe unification variable. The returned term is Type (U_Unif ?u). *)

unit -> Tac term (** Call the unifier on two terms. The returned boolean specifies whether unification was possible. When the tactic returns true, the terms have been unified, instantiating uvars as needed. When false, unification was not possible and no change to uvars occurs. *)
env -> t1:term -> t2:term -> Tac bool (** Similar to [unify_env], but allows for some guards to be raised during unification (see [t_trefl] for an explanation). Will add a new goal with the guard. *)

env -> t1:term -> t2:term -> Tac bool (** Check if [t1] matches [t2], i.e., whether [t2] can have its uvars instantiated into unifying with [t1]. When the tactic returns true, the terms have been unified, instantiating uvars as needed. When false, matching was not possible and no change to uvars occurs. *)

env -> t1:term -> t2:term -> Tac bool (** Launches an external process [prog] with arguments [args] and input [input] and returns the output. For security reasons, this can only be performed when the `--unsafe_tactic_exec` options was provided for the current F* invocation. The tactic will fail if this is not so. *)

string -> list string -> string -> Tac string (** Get a fresh bv of some name and type. The name is only useful for pretty-printing, since there is a fresh inaccessible integer within the bv too. *) (* val fresh_bv_named : string -> Tac bv *) (** Change the goal to another type, given that it is convertible to the current type. *)

typ -> Tac unit (** Get the current guard policy. The guard policy specifies what should be done when a VC arises internally from the tactic engine. Options are SMT (mark it as an SMT goal), Goal (add it as an extra goal) and Force (only allow trivial guards, that need no SMT. *)
unit -> TacRO guard_policy (** Set the current guard policy. See [get_guard_policy} for an explanation *)
guard_policy -> Tac unit (** [lax_on] returns true iff the current environment has the `--admit_smt_queries true` option set, and thus drops all verification conditions. *)
unit -> TacRO bool (** Admit the current goal and set the witness to the given term. Absolutely unsafe. Raises a warning. *)

term -> Tac unit (** Join the first two goals, which must be irrelevant, in a single one by finding a maximal prefix of their environment and reverting appropriately. Useful to minimize SMT queries that share internal obligations. *)
unit -> Tac unit (* Local metastate via a string-keyed map. [lget] fails if the found element is not typeable at the requested type. *)
#a:Type -> string -> TacRO a
#a:Type -> string -> a -> Tac unit (** Set the current set of active goals at will. Obligations remain in the implicits. *)
list goal -> Tac unit (** Set the current set of SMT goals at will. Obligations remain in the implicits. TODO: This is a really bad name, there's no special "SMT" about these goals. *)
list goal -> Tac unit (** [curms ()] returns the current (wall) time in milliseconds *)
unit -> TacRO int (** [set_urgency u] sets the urgency of error messages. Usually set just before raising an exception (see e.g. [fail_silently]). *)

int -> TacS unit (** [set_dump_failure b] controls whether the engine will dump out the proofstate if a tactic fails during exception. This is true by default, but can be disabled to get less verbosity. *)
bool -> TacS unit (** [string_to_term e s] runs the F* parser on the string [s] in the environment [e], and produces a term. *)

env -> string -> TacRO term (** [push_bv_dsenv e id] pushes a identifier [id] into the parsing environment of [e] an environment. It returns a new environment that has the identifier [id] along with its corresponding binding. *)

env -> string -> Tac (env & binding) (** Print a term via the pretty printer. This is considered effectful since 1) setting options can change the behavior of this function, and hence is not really pure; and 2) this function could expose details of the term representation that do not show up in the view, invalidating the pack_inspect_inv/inspeck_pack_inv lemmas. *)
term -> TacRO string (** Print a computation type via the pretty printer. See comment on [term_to_string]. *)
comp -> TacRO string (** Like term_to_string, but returns an unrendered pretty-printing document *)
term -> TacRO Pprint.document (** Like comp_to_string, but returns an unrendered pretty-printing document *)
comp -> TacRO Pprint.document (** Print a source range as a string *)
range -> TacRO string (** Runs the input tactic `f` with compat pre core setting `n`. It is an escape hatch for maintaining backward compatibility for code that breaks with the core typechecker. *)
#a:Type -> n:int -> f:(unit -> Tac a) -> Tac a (** Get the [vconfig], including fuel, ifuel, rlimit, etc, associated with the current goal. *)

unit -> TacRO vconfig (** Set the [vconfig], including fuel, ifuel, rlimit, etc, associated with the current goal. This vconfig will be used if the goal is attempted by SMT at the end of a tactic run. *)

vconfig -> Tac unit (** Attempt to solve the current goal with SMT immediately, and fail if it cannot be solved. The vconfig specifies fuels, limits, etc. The current goal's vconfig is ignored in favor of this one. *)
vconfig -> Tac unit (** This returns the free uvars that appear in a term. This is not a reflection primitive as it depends on the state of the UF graph. *)
term -> TacRO (list int) (* Return all k/v pairs in the state. The order is unspecified, do not rely on it. *)
unit -> TacRO (list (string & string)) (* Lookup a k/v pair in the --ext option state. The empty string is returned if the key was unset. *)

tactic_proof: let add_comm a b = ()
goal: val add_comm: a:int -> b:int -> Lemma (a + b == b + a)

tactic_proof: let add_assoc a b c = ()
goal: val add_assoc: a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma ((a + b) + c == a + (b + c))

tactic_proof: let mul_comm a b = ()
goal: val mul_comm: a:int -> b:int -> Lemma (a * b == b * a)

tactic_proof: let mul_assoc a b c = ()
goal: val mul_assoc: a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma ((a * b) * c == a * (b * c))

tactic_proof: let distributive a b c = ()
goal: val distributive: a:int -> b:int -> c:int -> Lemma (a * (b + c) == a * b + a * c)

tactic_proof: let add_zero_r a = ()
goal: val add_zero_r: a:int -> Lemma (a + 0 == a)

tactic_proof: let mul_one_r a = ()
goal: val mul_one_r: a:int -> Lemma (a * 1 == a)

tactic_proof: let mul_zero_r a = ()
goal: val mul_zero_r: a:int -> Lemma (a * 0 == 0)

tactic_proof: let rec pow_add b m n = if m = 0 then () else pow_add b (m - 1) n
goal: val pow_add: b:nat -> m:nat -> n:nat -> Lemma (ensures pow b (m + n) == pow b m * pow b n) (decreases m)

tactic_proof: let rec pow_mul b m n = if n = 0 then () else (pow_add b m (m * (n - 1)); pow_mul b m (n - 1))
goal: val pow_mul: b:nat -> m:nat -> n:nat -> Lemma (ensures pow b (m * n) == pow (pow b m) n) (decreases n)

tactic_proof: let div_exact a b = FStar.Math.Lemmas.lemma_div_exact a b
goal: val div_exact: a:nat -> b:pos -> Lemma (requires a % b == 0) (ensures a / b * b == a)

tactic_proof: let rec append_nil #a l = match l with | [] -> () | _::tl -> append_nil tl
goal: val append_nil: #a:Type -> l:list a -> Lemma (l @ [] == l)

tactic_proof: let rec length_append #a l1 l2 = match l1 with | [] -> () | _::tl -> length_append tl l2
goal: val length_append: #a:Type -> l1:list a -> l2:list a -> Lemma (length (l1 @ l2) == length l1 + length l2)

tactic_proof: let rec rev_rev #a l = match l with | [] -> () | hd::tl -> rev_append_rev tl [hd]; rev_rev tl
goal: val rev_rev: #a:Type -> l:list a -> Lemma (rev (rev l) == l)

tactic_proof: let rec map_append #a #b f l1 l2 = match l1 with | [] -> () | _::tl -> map_append f tl l2
goal: val map_append: #a:Type -> #b:Type -> f:(a -> b) -> l1:list a -> l2:list a -> Lemma (map f (l1 @ l2) == map f l1 @ map f l2)

tactic_proof: let rec length_map #a #b f l = match l with | [] -> () | _::tl -> length_map f tl
goal: val length_map: #a:Type -> #b:Type -> f:(a -> b) -> l:list a -> Lemma (length (map f l) == length l)

tactic_proof: let rec mem_append #a x l1 l2 = match l1 with | [] -> () | _::tl -> mem_append x tl l2
goal: val mem_append: #a:eqtype -> x:a -> l1:list a -> l2:list a -> Lemma (mem x (l1 @ l2) <==> mem x l1 || mem x l2)

tactic_proof: let rec filter_append #a f l1 l2 = match l1 with | [] -> () | hd::tl -> filter_append f tl l2
goal: val filter_append: #a:Type -> f:(a -> bool) -> l1:list a -> l2:list a -> Lemma (filter f (l1 @ l2) == filter f l1 @ filter f l2)

tactic_proof: let rec fold_left_append #a #b f init l1 l2 = match l1 with | [] -> () | hd::tl -> fold_left_append f (f init hd) tl l2
goal: val fold_left_append: #a:Type -> #b:Type -> f:(b -> a -> b) -> init:b -> l1:list a -> l2:list a -> Lemma (fold_left f init (l1 @ l2) == fold_left f (fold_left f init l1) l2)

tactic_proof: let rec for_all_append #a f l1 l2 = match l1 with | [] -> () | _::tl -> for_all_append f tl l2
goal: val for_all_append: #a:Type -> f:(a -> bool) -> l1:list a -> l2:list a -> Lemma (for_all f (l1 @ l2) <==> for_all f l1 && for_all f l2)
val aead_encrypt_decrypt: k:key -> n:nonce -> p:plaintext -> ad:aad -> Lemma (ensures (let c = aead_encrypt k n p ad in aead_decrypt k n c ad == Some p))
val hmac_verify: k:key -> m:msg -> t:tag -> Lemma (requires t == hmac k m) (ensures verify_hmac k m t == true)
val hash_collision_resistance: m1:msg -> m2:msg -> Lemma (requires m1 <> m2) (ensures hash m1 <> hash m2) [SMTPat (hash m1); SMTPat (hash m2)]
val kdf_extract_length: salt:bytes -> ikm:bytes -> Lemma (ensures length (kdf_extract salt ikm) == hash_length)
val chacha20_involutive: k:key -> n:nonce -> ctr:nat -> p:bytes -> Lemma (ensures chacha20 k n ctr (chacha20 k n ctr p) == p)

tactic_proof: let incr r = r := !r + 1
goal: val incr: r:ref int -> ST unit (requires fun h -> True) (ensures fun h0 _ h1 -> sel h1 r == sel h0 r + 1)

tactic_proof: let swap r1 r2 = let t = !r1 in r1 := !r2; r2 := t
goal: val swap: r1:ref int -> r2:ref int -> ST unit (requires fun h -> True) (ensures fun h0 _ h1 -> sel h1 r1 == sel h0 r2 /\ sel h1 r2 == sel h0 r1)

tactic_proof: let rec factorial n = if n = 0 then 1 else n * factorial (n - 1)
goal: val factorial: n:nat -> Tot (r:nat{r >= 1})

tactic_proof: let rec fibonacci n = if n <= 1 then n else fibonacci (n - 1) + fibonacci (n - 2)
goal: val fibonacci: n:nat -> Tot nat (decreases n)

tactic_proof: let alloc_and_free n = let r = alloc n in free r
goal: val alloc_and_free: n:nat -> ST unit (requires fun h -> True) (ensures fun h0 _ h1 -> modifies Set.empty h0 h1)

tactic_proof: let nat_positive n = n - 1
goal: val nat_positive: n:nat{n > 0} -> Tot (r:nat{r >= 0})

tactic_proof: let bounded_add a b = a + b
goal: val bounded_add: a:nat{a < 100} -> b:nat{b < 100} -> Tot (r:nat{r < 200})

tactic_proof: let safe_div a b = a / b
goal: val safe_div: a:int -> b:int{b <> 0} -> Tot int

tactic_proof: let vector_index #n v i = Seq.index v i
goal: val vector_index: #n:nat -> v:vector n -> i:nat{i < n} -> Tot elem
val sorted_insert: x:int -> l:list int{sorted l} -> Tot (r:list int{sorted r /\ length r == length l + 1})

tactic_proof: let non_empty_head #a l = match l with | hd::_ -> hd
goal: val non_empty_head: #a:Type -> l:list a{Cons? l} -> Tot a
val matrix_mult: #m:nat -> #n:nat -> #p:nat -> matrix m n -> matrix n p -> Tot (matrix m p)
val well_typed_eval: #t:typ -> e:exp{has_type e t} -> Tot (v:value{value_type v t})

tactic_proof: let rec gcd a b = if b = 0 then a else gcd b (a % b)
goal: val gcd: a:nat -> b:nat{a > 0 || b > 0} -> Tot (r:pos) (decreases b)
val collatz_steps: n:pos -> Tot nat (decreases %[n; if n = 1 then 0 else 1])

tactic_proof:
let rec mutual_even n = if n = 0 then true else mutual_odd (n - 1)
and mutual_odd n = if n = 0 then false else mutual_even (n - 1)
goal:
val mutual_even: n:nat -> Tot bool (decreases n)
val mutual_odd: n:nat -> Tot bool (decreases n)
